metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Claude Code exposes `/model <name>` as a slash command in the TUI; selecting a new model takes effect on the NEXT turn. Internally, the next assistant event's `message.model` field carries the new model name, exactly the same field the parser reads for first-turn identification. There is no separate 'model-changed' event; the change is implicit in the per-turn model field.

irrlicht's parser already calls `extractClaudeCodeModel` on every event and writes the result to `ev.ModelName`. The metrics aggregator's merge rule (see `MergeMetrics` in `session.go`) is 'latest non-unknown wins' for ModelName — line 627 keeps the old name sticky only when the new one is empty/unknown, so a genuine new model name from turn 2 overwrites turn 1's. `PerTurnContribution.Model` is also set per-turn (in `applyRequestIDContribution`), so the cost calculator attributes turn-1 tokens to model A and turn-2 tokens to model B.

Spec bullets:
- 'After turn 1: ModelName = <model A>' — first assistant event carries A in message.model; ModelName is set.
- 'After turn 2: ModelName = <model B> (latest wins)' — second assistant event carries B; the latest-wins merge updates the session row.
- 'TotalTokens after turn 2 > after turn 1 (accumulates across models)' — each turn's PerTurnContribution is added independently; the tokens accumulate regardless of model identity.

Note that the existing `model-switch` recipe in `scenarios.json` (line 290) is marked `aider`-only because aider's `/model` semantics are slightly different (and aider records model on a separate `> Model:` line). For claudecode the equivalent recipe is a new one — `/model <other-model>` between two `send` steps — and the assertions land on the same parser output.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why that's OK)

The `/model` command itself is a local-command wrapper (`<local-command-stdout>` etc.) that the parser explicitly skips in `isLocalCommandContent`. That is correct: the slash command is administrative; the observable signal is the next turn's model field.

There is no per-turn ModelName history exposed via the API today — the dashboard sees only the latest. The spec doesn't require historical per-turn model, only that turn-N's model is reflected when queried. Per-turn cost attribution via PerTurnContribution.Model is internal-only.
caveats: The recipe's between-turn step must be a TUI slash command, not a CLI flag: `claudecode` does not accept a mid-session model change via stdin in headless mode the same way. The driver may need to use tmux send-keys for /model.

Model name normalization (lowercasing, prefix stripping) can mask whether the user typed `claude-sonnet-4-5` vs `Claude Sonnet 4.5`. The recipe should compare against the normalized form, not the typed form.
scenarios.json's existing model-switch entry only declares the aider branch; the claudecode branch needs to be added for this cell to be recordable.

Capture status (RESOLVED 2026-05-29, supersedes the #405 known_failing note): the recording now passes 6/6 and the transcript carries two distinct message.model values (claude-opus-4-8 on turn 1, claude-sonnet-4-6 on turn 2). The old 4/6 failure was a RECIPE flaw, not a daemon bug: the recipe blind-navigated the /model picker (keys Down+Enter), which neither pinned a deterministic model A (the interactive driver has no --model flag, so turn 1 ran on the account default) nor reliably selected model B. Fix: pin model A via the recipe's --settings (model: opus), switch with the inline-argument form /model sonnet (no picker), and answer claude's mid-conversation cache-confirmation dialog with keys 1 + Enter (a pre-turn switch shows no dialog, but a mid-session one does — and without the confirm the next send's keystrokes are swallowed). A trailing /model opus restores the account default. The yes/full/ready axes were always correct; only the capture path needed the recipe fix. known_failing removed from expected.jsonl.

sources.note: applyRequestIDContribution sets PerTurnContribution.Model from extractClaudeCodeModel — per-turn model attribution is built in.
MergeMetrics ModelName rule: latest non-empty/non-unknown wins, so turn 2's new model replaces turn 1's.
Feature: Model switch mid-session — two turns, model A then model B, latest wins, tokens accumulate across models.
model-switch scenario exists for aider; claudecode branch needs to be added with a similar /model script.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
no other claudecode session uses the recipe's UUID

both model A (the recipe pins the `opus` alias via --settings, e.g. resolves to claude-opus-4-8) and model B (the `sonnet` switch target, e.g. claude-sonnet-4-6) are available on the maintainer's account
the running claude CLI accepts the INLINE-ARGUMENT form `/model <alias>` (e.g. `/model sonnet`) which switches directly with no arrow-key picker (confirmed claude 2.1.156)
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/.

The recipe's settings blob pins `model: opus` so claude launches under model A deterministically — turn 1 is attributed to A regardless of the account's persisted global default. This is the load-bearing determinism fix (the prior recipe launched on whatever default the account had, so turn 1 and the picker-selected turn 2 could both be the SAME model — the cause of the old 4/6 known_failing).

Between turns the driver sends `/model sonnet` as a single `slash` step (inline alias, NO arrow-key picker — no model-navigation `keys`, no driver_gap). MID-CONVERSATION, claude then shows a one-key CACHE-CONFIRMATION dialog ('This conversation is cached for the current model. Switching to Sonnet 4.6 means the full history gets re-read... 1. Yes, switch / 2. No, go back') — NOT shown for a pre-turn switch. The recipe answers it with `keys 1` + `keys Enter` to confirm; the footer then flips to Sonnet (prints `Set model to Sonnet 4.6`). WITHOUT this confirmation the switch is left pending and the next `send` text is swallowed by the dialog filter (the exact bug that produced the old single-model 4/6 recording). Turn 2 then runs under model B.

A final `slash` step `/model opus` (also followed by `keys 1` + `keys Enter` — the cache dialog fires for the restore too, since there is now even more history) restores the account's global default model after the recording: claude's /model persists the choice as the default for new sessions in ~/.claude/settings.json (it writes there even when --settings is active), so without this restore the recording would leave the user's default flipped to sonnet. The recording's observable assertions are all complete before this restoring step.
Compare ModelName against the NORMALIZED form (lowercased, provider-prefix stripped) — not the typed alias (assessment caveat 2).

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: turn-one
Reply with exactly the word: turn-two

verify: events.jsonl shows two ready→working→ready cycles on the same UUID (one per turn).

transcript.jsonl carries TWO DISTINCT message.model values — model A on turn 1's assistant line (claude-opus-4-8) and model B on turn 2's (claude-sonnet-4-6). This is the load-bearing evidence the switch happened; the prior 4/6 recording had a single model.
events.jsonl carries model_name = <model A normalized> at the t1_end metric snapshot and = <model B normalized> at t2_end (MergeMetrics latest-non-unknown-wins promotes B).

events.jsonl shows NO state_transition to working or waiting during the /model switch window between t1_end and t2_start (the <local-command> wrappers are filtered by isLocalCommandContent — the inline /model does no LLM round-trip).
PerTurnContribution.Model for turn 1 = model A; for turn 2 = model B (per-turn cost attribution).
SessionMetrics.TotalTokens after t2_end > TotalTokens after t1_end (accumulates across both models).
the account's global default model is restored to opus after the run (the trailing `/model opus` step).
recipe.notes: FIXED 2026-05-29 (was 4/6 known_failing, issue #405): the prior recipe opened /model and blind-navigated the picker with `keys Down` + `keys Enter`, which did NOT land on a different model — the captured transcript had a SINGLE model (claude-opus-4-7) so phase t2_end (ModelName = model B) failed and cascaded. ROOT CAUSE was twofold: (1) the driver launches claude with only --settings + --session-id, it has NO --model flag, so turn 1 ran on the account's persisted global default, not a pinned model A; and (2) the picker Down+Enter selection is positional/non-deterministic. BOTH are fixed by abandoning the picker entirely: claude's /model accepts an INLINE ALIAS ARGUMENT (`/model sonnet`, `/model opus`) that switches DIRECTLY with no picker (verified live in claude 2.1.156: footer flips and it prints `Set model to Sonnet 4.6`), so the switch is sendable via the existing `slash` step — NO `keys` and NO driver_gap. Model A is pinned deterministically by the recipe's settings blob (`model: opus`) so turn 1 is opus regardless of the account default; the mid-session `/model sonnet` makes turn 2 sonnet — two distinct message.model values (claude-opus-4-8, claude-sonnet-4-6). CACHE-CONFIRMATION DIALOG (the second half of the original bug): when /model is issued MID-CONVERSATION (history already cached), claude does NOT switch directly — it pops a one-key dialog 'This conversation is cached for the current model. Switching to Sonnet 4.6 means the full history gets re-read... 1. Yes, switch / 2. No, go back'. The recipe answers it with `keys 1` + `keys Enter` after each /model slash. The FIRST broken re-record (transcript had a single model + no turn-two) was this dialog eating the turn-two `send` keystrokes: the slash switched, the dialog appeared, and turn-two's text+Enter landed IN the dialog (Enter confirmed 'Yes' so the switch completed, but turn 2 was never prompted). A pre-turn /model (no history) shows no dialog — which is why the live probe switched cleanly but the in-recording mid-session switch did not. Both /model slashes here are mid-conversation, so both get the `keys 1`+`Enter` confirm (the restoring /model opus too). WHY NO wait_turn after the /model slash or after turn 2: the driver's `slash` step calls step_send which BUMPS the expected-turn counter exactly like `send`, but /model produces no assistant turn — so after the slash EXPECTED is off-by-one and a turn-2 wait_turn would block forever waiting for a turn that never completes. Same compromise as the pi/aider model-switch recipes: turn 1 uses wait_turn (counter still in sync), the slash + turn 2 are covered by a fixed 12s sleep (a one-word `turn-two` reply completes and settles working→ready well within that window — verified live ~3s). WHY the trailing `/model opus`: claude's /model persists the alias as the global default for new sessions in ~/.claude/settings.json EVEN with --settings active (verified live: a /model sonnet flipped the global model key while the session's --settings file stayed opus), so the recording restores the user's opus default as its last step; all six spec phases are observable before it. The /model command itself is a <local-command-stdout> wrapper the parser skips in isLocalCommandContent, so the model_switch_window phase sees state stay ready with no working/waiting flicker. The model A→B promotion lands via MergeMetrics' latest-non-empty/non-unknown-wins rule (session.go L627-628) and PerTurnContribution.Model is set per-turn in applyRequestIDContribution. What differs between runs: token counts/cost, UUID/PID/timestamps, and the exact alias→version mapping (opus/sonnet resolve to whatever the account's latest of each family is — assert change + presence, not a hardcoded pair, per issue #440). What must NOT differ: two distinct message.model values (one per turn, A≠B), two ready→working→ready arcs on one UUID, no waiting/working during the switch window, and ModelName = B (latest) at t2_end.
